name: Yoghurt Sourdough Starter
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat milk to 100°F on a thermometer. Remove from heat and stir in yoghurt.
2. Pour into a scalded glass jar or bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and place in a warm location for 18 hours. The consistency will be like thin yoghurt.
3. Stir in flour until well blended, cover again with plastic wrap, and pierce with a fork to release gases. Place in a warm draft-free location at an even 85°F for 2 days. It should have a strong sourdough smell and show bubbles.
4. Refrigerate until ready to use. When replenishing the starter, add lukewarm milk instead of water.
